chapter_title.en: Chapter 49 – The Found and the Lost Properties

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/114)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (19/114)
text: مَنْ أَصَابَ مَالًا أَوْ بَعِيراً فِي فَلَاةٍ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ قَدْ كَلَّتْ وَ قَامَتْ وَ سَيَّبَهَا صَاحِبُهَا مِمَّا لَمْ يَتْبَعْهُ فَأَخَذَهَا غَيْرُهُ فَأَقَامَ عَلَيْهَا وَ أَنْفَقَ نَفَقَةً حَتَّى أَحْيَاهَا مِنَ الْكَلَالِ وَ مِنَ الْمَوْتِ فَهِيَ لَهُ وَ لَا سَبِيلَ لَهُ عَلَيْهَا وَ إِنَّمَا هِيَ مِثْلُ الشَّيْ‏ءِ الْمُبَاحِ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, and Sahl Bin Ziyad, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abdullah Bin Sinan,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The one who comes across some wealth, or a camel in the wilderness from the earth which was exhausted and its owner had abandoned from what he did not pursue it, so someone else finds it and stands to look after it and spends expenditure until he revives it from the exhaustion and from the death, so it is for him, and there is no way for him (the previous owner) upon it. But rather it is like the ownerless thing’.
en.sarwar: 13. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from and Sahl ibn Ziyad from ibn Mahbub from ‘ Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If one finds a certain amount of goods, or a camel in wilderness which has become tired has stopped walking and its owner has abandoned it; someone other than the owner then takes it, tends to it and incurs expenses to revive it from tiredness and death; it then is for him. He (the owner) has no reason to claim it. It then is like a lawful thing for him (the finder).”’
